Options.Label: MaxBacktrackSteps
Options.Description: The maximum number of backtracking steps the algorithm will take before starting from scratch again (Attempts).

Options.Label: MaxAttempts
Options.Description: The maximum number of attempts the algorithm will make to generate a valid layout before giving up. If the algorithm fails to generate a valid layout within the maximum number of attempts, it will stop and output whatever it has generated so far.

Options.Label: New Attempt Behavior [Coming Soon]
Options.Description:
Behavior when starting a new attempt after reaching the maximum number of backtracking steps.

- RETRY will simply start over with the same settings until MaxAttempts is reached.

- The SIMPLIFY_X% options will remove a sub percentage of base tiles from the pool after each failed attempt.

- The SIMPLIFY_INCRIMENTAL option will increase the percentage unitl it successfully generates a layout or reaches 90% simplification. This is the recommended option if you want to ensure a layout is generated, but don't care about the quality of the layout.
